mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, which increases consumer purchasing power and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135902.0 thousand as of June 2013
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in < 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.15% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth, real rates turn negative, incentivizing equity allocation over cash and bonds, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL at 231.831 as of mid-2013
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation with positive inflation
failure_condition: Negative Rank IC over any 3-year window when trigger is active, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month moving average of housing starts (HOUST) crossing above 900,000 units signals accelerating residential construction demand, which multiplies through supply chains and employment, producing positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 914.0 thousand as of May 2013
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in < 60% of crossover events.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and future earnings headwinds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward-looking equity prices adjust.
failure_condition: Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative 5-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both accelerating simultaneously signal broad-based demand strength across consumer and construction sectors, creating a multiplier effect that drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 422794.0 million and HOUST at 914.0 thousand as of June 2013
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window when both conditions are met, or positive 20-day returns occur in < 65% of joint trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Rank IC > -0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative 60-day returns occur in < 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2% annualized while the federal funds rate remains at zero signals stagnation risk, prompting equity repricing downward over 20 trading days as recession probability increases.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13725.7 billion (Q1 2013) and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of June 2013
failure_condition: Rank IC > -0.05 over any 2-year rolling window when trigger is active, or negative 20-day returns occur in < 58% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerating above 1.5% month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows steadily signals synchronized supply-side and labor productivity gains, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as profit margins expand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 1.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change > 0.15%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.6709 and PAYEMS at 135902.0 thousand as of June 2013
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window when both conditions are met, or positive 5-day returns occur in < 62% of joint trigger events.
